questionHtml: The genes for cystic fibrosis and blood group are not linked. Two parents are heterozygous for cystic fibrosis. One parent has blood group O and the other has blood group AB. Using a Punnett square, determine the probability that their child will have both cystic fibrosis and blood group A.
markschemeHtml:
a. correct parental genotypes shown / Ff ii, FfIA IB 

b. Punnett square with correct gametes shown 

c. correct probability:  1 8

                                   OR
                                   12.5%

Must use blood type symbols IB IA and i. No specific letters required to represent cystic fibrosis allele though dominant and recessive must be apparent.
